## [48:27 - 51:30]

call reviews. And that's because of what's called the principle of specificity. Okay. Which basically is the more specific the feedback and the more specific the training, the better the adaptation to that training. So for instance, you can role play with somebody on a, on a morning meeting and you can kind of diagnose and ask them some questions and say, okay, well, here's what I did with them differently. But if you actually review the call and you, like we do yours, and then you voiceover, you pause the call and you say, Hey, look how you said this. Your tonality was this way. Here's what I really would have said. Here's how to role play differently. Here's four or five questions you should ask instead. That's going to create the distinction because in real time, they're able to actually see what they're doing. Okay. It's like a football coach.
